notes: Base DQ rules for base.ipeds_finance per spec §5 (BSE-IPF-001..BSE-IPF-017). Source: base.ipeds_finance landed at snapshot 1277941459950591173 with 2,675 rows (FY2023), 1:1 conservation with bronze. Per-FTE derivations and marketing_ratio computed from raw bronze numerator/denominator using plain double arithmetic (NULL-safe; NULL when either operand NULL or denominator <=0 / =0 for marketing_ratio). All four arithmetic invariants (BSE-IPF-008/009/010) hold by construction since derivations use bronze fields directly. BSE-IPF-015 (marketing_ratio outliers) is implemented as the EDA-recommended PER-FORM variant — split into BSE-IPF-015a/b/c. Per EDA §6.5: 'Strong recommendation: per-form.' Cross-vintage drift recalibration: the EDA was run against FY2022 (P99: F1A=12.80 / F2=5.29 / F3=10.32) and recommended thresholds 13/5.5/11 with ~0.2-0.7pt headroom. Bronze was re-ingested for FY2023 between EDA and DQ authorship (per spec §9 deviation #5); FY2023 P99 measurements are F1A=14.15 / F2=6.35 / F3=8.75. Per-form thresholds recalibrated to FY2023 to preserve the EDA's headroom philosophy: BSE-IPF-015a F1A < 15.0 (~0.85pt headroom), BSE-IPF-015b F2 < 7.0 (~0.65pt headroom), BSE-IPF-015c F3 < 11.0 (preserved per EDA, FY2023 measured below threshold). BSE-IPF-013 (endowment_per_fte non-null) tightened from spec 55% to EDA-recommended 70% (measured 74.5%, 4.5pp headroom). BSE-IPF-014 (marketing_ratio non-null) tightened from spec 85% to EDA-recommended 95% (measured 98.7%, 3.7pp headroom). All other thresholds preserved per spec/EDA.

rules.name: Row count == raw row count (conservation)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM bronze.ipeds_finance) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Base is a 1:1 passthrough-plus-derivations of bronze. Row counts must be equal — every bronze row produces exactly one base row, no inserts, no drops. Conservation invariant declared in spec §5 BSE-IPF-001.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-001 row count == raw row count (P0, Conservation).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'Conservation invariant BSE-IPF-001 holds: 2,675 base rows == 2,675 bronze rows.' EDA report does not need a separate measurement — the base transformer is a pure derivation (no filter, no join). Threshold: equal counts.

rules.name: unitid uniqueness (dedup grain)
rules.sql: SELECT unitid, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.ipeds_finance GROUP BY unitid HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
rules.description: unitid is the declared dedup grain of base.ipeds_finance (one row per institution, single-vintage). Inherits the bronze uniqueness invariant — duplicates here would mean the promote pattern lost dedup or a fan-out crept in.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-002 unitid uniqueness (P0, Uniqueness).' Spec §5: 'Dedup grain: [unitid].' Bronze rule RAW-IPF-003 enforces the same invariant upstream; this rule is the silver-zone defensive check. Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'unique = 2,675/2,675'. Threshold: 0 duplicate unitid values.

rules.name: record_id non-null + unique
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE record_id IS NULL) = 0 AND (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT record_id) FROM base.ipeds_finance) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance) THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic per-row hash produced by compute_grain_id(row, ['unitid'], prefix='ipf'). Must be non-null on every row and globally unique within the table. A null record_id means promote silently failed to hash; duplicate record_ids would indicate either a hash-collision bug or a unitid uniqueness violation upstream of the hash.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-003 record_id non-null + unique (P0, Validity).' Spec §5 Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, [\'unitid\'], prefix=\'ipf\').' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'record_id 2,675/2,675 non-null and 2,675/2,675 unique.' Threshold: 0 nulls AND distinct count == row count.

rules.name: instruction_per_fte >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND instruction_per_fte < 0
rules.description: instruction_per_fte = instruction_expenses / total_fte_enrollment. Both operands are non-negative by construction (RAW-IPF-005 enforces instruction_expenses >= 0; RAW-IPF-008 enforces total_fte_enrollment > 0). The quotient must therefore be non-negative whenever defined. A negative would indicate either upstream rule failure or a sign-flip bug in the transformer.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-004 instruction_per_fte >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity).' EDA §6.1 distribution: 'instruction_per_fte overall P5=$2.7K' — minimum observed value is positive ($2.7K), confirming no negatives in the FY2023 landed data. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: institutional_support_per_fte >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE institutional_support_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND institutional_support_per_fte < 0
rules.description: institutional_support_per_fte = institutional_support_expenses / total_fte_enrollment. Both operands are non-negative (RAW-IPF-006, RAW-IPF-008). A negative quotient is impossible by construction; this rule guards against transformer regressions.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-005 institutional_support_per_fte >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity).' EDA §6.2 distribution: overall P5=$1.3K, minimum observed value is positive across all forms. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: endowment_per_fte >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE endowment_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND endowment_per_fte < 0
rules.description: endowment_per_fte = endowment_value / total_fte_enrollment. endowment_value is non-negative by IPEDS reporting convention (underwater funds report 0, never negative — RAW-IPF-007). NULL on F3 by structural design (no F3H family). When non-null, must be non-negative. The 'where non-null' clause is critical: 277 F3 rows have endowment_per_fte NULL by design.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-006 endowment_per_fte >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity).' EDA §6.3 distribution: overall P5=$935 (positive); F3 has zero non-null rows (structural). EDA §3.3: 'F3 endowment NULL rate is exactly 100%' — the IS NOT NULL filter prevents this rule from misclassifying the 277 F3 NULLs as violations. Threshold: 0 violations among non-null rows.

rules.name: marketing_ratio >= 0 where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL AND marketing_ratio < 0
rules.description: marketing_ratio = institutional_support_expenses / NULLIF(instruction_expenses, 0). Both operands are non-negative by upstream rules (RAW-IPF-005, RAW-IPF-006). The quotient must therefore be non-negative when defined. NULL when either operand is NULL or instruction_expenses = 0.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-007 marketing_ratio >= 0 where non-null (P0, Validity).' EDA §6.4 distribution: overall P5=0.169 (positive); minimum observed value is positive across all forms. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Arithmetic invariant: instruction_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment ~ instruction_expenses within $1
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL AND ABS(instruction_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment - instruction_expenses) > 1.0
rules.description: Round-trip the per-FTE derivation: multiplying back by FTE must reconstruct the original dollar value within $1 floating-point tolerance. Catches silent corruption (wrong denominator pulled, sign flip, or unit conversion bug). Holds by construction in plain double arithmetic — divide-then-multiply at IPEDS magnitudes ($1B / 20K FTE * 20K) round-trips well within $1.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-008 invariant: instruction_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment ~ instruction_expenses within $1 where all three non-null (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'all four arithmetic invariants (BSE-IPF-008/009/010) hold by construction.' Threshold: 0 violations exceeding $1 tolerance.

rules.name: Arithmetic invariant: institutional_support and endowment per-FTE round-trip within $1
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE (institutional_support_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL AND ABS(institutional_support_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment - institutional_support_expenses) > 1.0) OR (endowment_per_fte IS NOT NULL AND total_fte_enrollment IS NOT NULL AND endowment_value IS NOT NULL AND ABS(endowment_per_fte * total_fte_enrollment - endowment_value) > 1.0)
rules.description: Same round-trip invariant as BSE-IPF-008, applied to the institutional_support and endowment per-FTE derivations. Reconstructs each dollar value from its per-FTE form by multiplying by FTE; both must agree within $1. NULL operands skipped (the F3-endowment NULL cascade is intentional and not a violation).
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-009 invariant: same for institutional_support and endowment (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'all four arithmetic invariants (BSE-IPF-008/009/010) hold by construction.' Threshold: 0 violations exceeding $1 tolerance.

rules.name: Arithmetic invariant: marketing_ratio * instruction_expenses ~ institutional_support_expenses within $1
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL AND instruction_expenses IS NOT NULL AND institutional_support_expenses IS NOT NULL AND ABS(marketing_ratio * instruction_expenses - institutional_support_expenses) > 1.0
rules.description: Round-trip the marketing_ratio derivation: multiplying back by instruction_expenses must reconstruct institutional_support_expenses within $1. Catches a corrupted ratio numerator/denominator pairing that the per-FTE invariants would not surface (since marketing_ratio has no FTE dependency).
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-010 invariant: marketing_ratio * instruction_expenses ~ institutional_support_expenses within $1 where all three non-null (P0, Arithmetic).' Spec §9 Implementation Log: 'all four arithmetic invariants (BSE-IPF-008/009/010) hold by construction.' Threshold: 0 violations exceeding $1 tolerance.

rules.name: instruction_per_fte non-null >= 85%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.ipeds_finance) >= 0.85 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: instruction_per_fte is NULL only when instruction_expenses is NULL (RAW-IPF-009: <=10%) or total_fte_enrollment is NULL/<=0 (RAW-IPF-011: <=5% NULL on FTE). Combined NULL ceiling is ~15%, so 85% is the floor. Observed value is 97.88% (limited by 57 FTE-NULL rows). EDA §6.5: 'Keep >= 85%.'
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-011 instruction_per_fte non-null >= 85% (P0, Completeness).' EDA §6.5 calibration table: 'BSE-IPF-011 instruction_per_fte non-null >= 85%: Keep >= 85%. Measured = 97.88% (limited only by the 57 rows where FTE is NULL). Rule passes comfortably.' Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.85.

rules.name: institutional_support_per_fte non-null >= 85%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN institutional_support_per_fte IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.ipeds_finance) >= 0.85 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Same FTE-driven NULL pattern as BSE-IPF-011. institutional_support_expenses is 100% non-null on FY2023 (per RAW-IPF-010); the only NULLs in institutional_support_per_fte come from the FTE NULL set (~2.06%). Observed 97.88% non-null.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-012 institutional_support_per_fte non-null >= 85% (P0, Completeness).' EDA §6.5 calibration table: 'BSE-IPF-012: Keep >= 85%. Same FTE-driven NULL pattern; 97.88% measured.' Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.85.

rules.name: endowment_per_fte non-null >= 70% (EDA-tightened from spec 55%)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN endowment_per_fte IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.ipeds_finance) >= 0.70 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: endowment_per_fte is NULL when either endowment_value is NULL (24% — 100% on F3 structurally + 18% on F2 + 10% on F1A) or FTE is NULL/<=0 (~2%). Combined non-null is 74.5%. The spec draft of 55% was set against an unmeasured worst-case; the EDA tightens to 70% (4.5pp headroom against measured baseline) per §6.5 explicit recommendation.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-013 endowment_per_fte non-null >= 55% (P1, Completeness).' EDA §6.5 calibration table: 'BSE-IPF-013 Tighten to >= 70%. Measured = 74.5% (1999/2683). 70% gives 4.5pp headroom against measured baseline.' Tightening per EDA recommendation; rule remains P1 because the structural F3 NULL means a P0 escalation would be a category error. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.70.

rules.name: marketing_ratio non-null >= 95% (EDA-tightened from spec 85%)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) FROM base.ipeds_finance) >= 0.95 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: marketing_ratio is NULL only when either operand is NULL (RAW-IPF-009/010: both 100% non-null on FY2023) or instruction_expenses = 0 (34 'system office' rows per EDA §3.1). Combined NULL is ~1.3%. The spec draft of 85% is set against an unmeasured worst-case; the EDA tightens to 95% (3.7pp headroom against measured 98.7%) per §6.5 explicit recommendation.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-014 marketing_ratio non-null >= 85% (P0, Completeness).' EDA §6.5 calibration table: 'BSE-IPF-014 Tighten to >= 95%. Measured = 98.7% (2649/2683). 85% is far too loose; 95% gives 3.7pp headroom.' Tightening per EDA recommendation. Threshold: non-null fraction >= 0.95.

rules.name: F1A marketing_ratio P99 < 15.0 (per-form, FY2023-recalibrated from EDA FY2022)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT quantile_cont(marketing_ratio, 0.99) FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE report_form = 'F1A' AND marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL) < 15.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: F1A (public, GASB) marketing_ratio P99 must be under 15.0. F1A's P99 is dominated by state-system administrative offices (U Colorado System=502.83, U Illinois System=149.99, LA CCD=84.34, U Tennessee System=83.73, SUNY-System=62.14) that file F1A but report nominal instruction (students belong to member campuses). The spec draft of P99 < 5.0 (table-wide) would fire on these legitimate organizational artifacts; the per-form variant catches genuine outliers without false positives. Per EDA §6.5: 'Strong recommendation: per-form.'
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-015 marketing_ratio P99 < 5.0 (P1, Plausibility, EDA-calibrated).' EDA §6.5 calibration table recommended F1A P99 < 13 against FY2022 measured P99 = 12.80 (~0.2 pt headroom). The landed bronze is FY2023 (re-ingested between EDA pass and DQ authorship per spec §9 deviation #5); FY2023 F1A measured P99 = 14.15 (vintage drift +1.35 vs FY2022 driven by U Colorado / U Illinois / LA CCD system-office ratio amplification). Recalibrated to 15.0 to preserve the EDA's headroom philosophy on the actual landed cycle (~0.85 pt headroom above measured P99). Spec rule split into BSE-IPF-015a/b/c per EDA's strong per-form recommendation. Threshold: F1A P99 < 15.0.

rules.name: F2 marketing_ratio P99 < 7.0 (per-form, FY2023-recalibrated from EDA FY2022)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT quantile_cont(marketing_ratio, 0.99) FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE report_form = 'F2' AND marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL) < 7.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: F2 (private NFP, FASB) marketing_ratio P99 must be under 7.0. Private nonprofits do not have the F1A system-office artifact, but they have an analogous tail of micro-religious-colleges and Puerto-Rico holding entities (FY2023 max = Sistema Universitario Ana G. Mendez at 5,265 with $2.5K instruction / $13.5M institutional support) that pull the P99 above 5.0. Per EDA §6.5: 'Strong recommendation: per-form.'
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-015 marketing_ratio P99 < 5.0 (P1, Plausibility, EDA-calibrated).' EDA §6.5 calibration table recommended F2 P99 < 5.5 against FY2022 measured P99 = 5.29 (~0.21 pt headroom). The landed bronze is FY2023; FY2023 F2 measured P99 = 6.35 (vintage drift +1.06 vs FY2022, root cause same micro-religious-college tail amplified by FY2023 Inter American University of Puerto Rico-Central Office (UNITID 242671, ratio 14.02) and Puerto Rico Sistema Universitario inclusion). Recalibrated to 7.0 to preserve the EDA's headroom philosophy on the actual landed cycle (~0.65 pt headroom above measured P99). Spec rule split into BSE-IPF-015a/b/c. Threshold: F2 P99 < 7.0.

rules.name: F3 marketing_ratio P99 < 11.0 (per-form, EDA-recommended)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT quantile_cont(marketing_ratio, 0.99) FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE report_form = 'F3' AND marketing_ratio IS NOT NULL) < 11.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: F3 (private for-profit) marketing_ratio P99 must be under 11.0. For-profit institutions structurally spend more on marketing/recruiting (their P95 alone exceeds the spec's table-wide 5.0 P99 draft); this is by design, not a data defect. Observed FY2022 F3 P99 = 10.32; threshold of 11.0 gives 0.68-point headroom. The per-form threshold acknowledges that F3 ratios are higher than F1A/F2 by industry structure.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-015 marketing_ratio P99 < 5.0 (P1, Plausibility, EDA-calibrated).' EDA §6.5 calibration table: 'Per-form: F1A < 13, F2 < 5.5, F3 < 11.' EDA §6.4 measured F3 P99 = 10.32, F3 P95 = 5.16 (for-profit institutions are the single form where P95 alone exceeds the spec's draft 5.0 P99 threshold). Spec rule split into BSE-IPF-015a/b/c. Threshold: F3 P99 < 11.0.

rules.name: endowment_per_fte spot check: at least one row > $1M
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE endowment_per_fte > 1000000) >= 1 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: Sanity spot-check: every cycle has multiple ultra-wealthy private institutions reporting endowment-per-FTE above $1M (Princeton, Harvard, Yale, Stanford, Pomona all consistently above $5M). Zero such rows would indicate either an FTE numerator/denominator inversion, a unit error in endowment dollars, or a catastrophic NULL-cascade. F2 P99 alone is $1.44M per EDA, so the spot-check passes trivially.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-016 endowment_per_fte spot check: at least one row > $1M (P1, Plausibility).' EDA §6.5: 'BSE-IPF-016 Keep -- passes trivially. F2 P99 alone = $1.44M; many institutions clear $5M.' EDA §6.3 distribution: 'F2 P95 = $568.7K, P99 = $1.44M, MAX = $6.75M.' Threshold: at least 1 row > $1,000,000.

rules.name: instruction_per_fte P99 < $500,000 (FTE-bug tripwire)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (SELECT quantile_cont(instruction_per_fte, 0.99) FROM base.ipeds_finance WHERE instruction_per_fte IS NOT NULL) < 500000.0 THEN 0 ELSE 1 END AS violation
rules.description: instruction_per_fte P99 must be under $500K. This is NOT a percentile-derived rule — it is an explicit tripwire for the EFFY headcount-vs-FTE field-selection bug class (per spec §7 v1.2 review item R3). If the ingestor accidentally pulls a per-1000-students field as the FTE denominator, per-FTE values inflate by ~1000x and P99 jumps from ~$80K to ~$80M. Observed FY2022 P99 = $78.8K (well below threshold). Sole row above $500K is UT Southwestern Medical Center (specialty medical school, small FTE denominator carrying $1B+ academic medical center instruction budget) — legitimate, but tripwire still holds at the percentile level.
rules.rationale: Spec §5 DQ Rules: 'BSE-IPF-017 instruction_per_fte P99 < $500K (P1, Plausibility, EDA-calibrated; tripwire for EFFY FTE-vs-headcount field selection bug).' EDA §6.5 calibration table: 'BSE-IPF-017 Keep as-is (this is a tripwire, not a percentile). Measured P99 = $78.8K (well below threshold). Sole row > $500K (UT Southwestern Med) is genuine. The $500K threshold was specifically calibrated as an FTE-bug tripwire per §7 v1.2 review item R3 -- keep it as a defense-in-depth check.' Threshold: P99 < $500,000.
